   DataResult preview_and_read_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {

        // Create a buffer_adaptor from the non_const_buffer
        hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::span<uint8_t>> adaptor(non_const_buffer);

            return result;
        }

        // Read the item_count

        stream.read(&item_count, 1);
        if (!stream.good()) {
            return result;
        }

            if (stream.read_max() < item_count * sizeof(uint32_t)) {
                return result;
            }

                uint32_t value;
                stream.read(value);

                float value;
                stream.read(value);
                if (!stream.good()) {
                    return result;
                }
                result.float_items.push_back(value);
            }
        }

        return result;
   }

   - Then create a vector of uint8_t with size data_length and read data_length bytes into it.

 However, note that the `read` method of dynamic_buffer advances the read cursor. So after reading the header, the next read will be the data.

   2. Create a result vector (which will accumulate the data we read).
   3. While the adaptor has data (size() > 0):
        a. Determine the current read chunk size: min(chunk_size, current size)
        b. Create a temporary buffer (vector<uint8_t>) of that size.
        c. Read into the temporary buffer using the read() method of the adaptor.
        d. Append the temporary buffer to the result vector.
   4. Return the result vector.

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <string>
#include <string_view>

struct MeasurementData {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::vector<float> temperatures;
    double pressure;
    
    void serialise(auto& adaptor) const {
        adaptor.write(sensor_id);
        uint32_t size = static_cast<uint32_t>(temperatures.size());
        adaptor.write(hexi::endian::conditional_reverse(size, std::endian::native, std::endian::little));
        for (auto t : temperatures) {
            float converted = hexi::endian::conditional_reverse(t, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
            adaptor.write(converted);
        }
        double converted_pressure = hexi::endian::conditional_reverse(pressure, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
        adaptor.write(converted_pressure);
    }
    
    void serialise(auto& adaptor) {
        adaptor.read(sensor_id);
        uint32_t size;
        adaptor.read(size);
        temperatures.resize(size);
        for (auto& t : temperatures) {
            float temp;
            adaptor.read(temp);
            hexi::endian::little_to_native_inplace(temp);
            t = temp;
        }
        double p;
        adaptor.read(p);
        hexi::endian::little_to_native_inplace(p);
        pressure = p;
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_measurement_data(const MeasurementData& data) {
    hexi::Buffer buffer;
    hexi::BinaryStream stream(buffer);
    stream.serialise(data);
    return buffer.data();
}

MeasurementData deserialize_measurement_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::Buffer buf(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
    hexi::BinaryStream stream(buf);
    MeasurementData data;
    stream.deserialise(data);
    return data;
}

struct SensorInfo {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::string_view sensor_name;
};

SensorInfo extract_sensor_info(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::Buffer buf(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
    hexi::BinaryStream stream(buf);
    
    std::string_view sensor_name = stream.view();
    stream.enforce_read_bounds(sizeof(uint32_t));
    uint32_t sensor_id;
